"'''A Tale from the Dark Side'''" was a short story by [[George R. Strayton]], originally published in the ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' by [[West End Games]]. It was later reprinted in the [[Star Wars Insider 88|''Star Wars Insider'' 88]] online supplement on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]. The story takes place in [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]].

==Plot summary==
[[Vara Nreem]], a Human, female Jedi Knight travels to [[Krayiss II|Krayiss Two]] to study [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings at a hidden [[Sith temple/Legends|Sith Temple]] without falling to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. She planned to pretend a position of neutrality in order to gain entrance to the temple. The site of the temple was masked by a black [[Krayiss Obelisk|obelisk]] protruding from the ground. Using chants she had learned from the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]], she began a ritual in which she hoped would reveal the entrance to the banks of knowledge.

As Vara finished the ritual the Sith library rose up from below the surface of the ground, the obelisk standing atop it as a spire. Vara's entrance was blocked by a Sith spirit. The spirit saw through Vara's scheme and mocked her ignorance of the power of the dark side, revealing the spirits of those who had come before her. Vara recognized the images as Jedi Masters, trapped in their own unsuccessful attempts to do as Vara had planned.

Vara drew her lightsaber to protect herself from the spirit who wished to claim her; the spirit attacked and her blows passed harmlessly through it. Before Vara died she wished that she could warn other Jedi away from sharing a similar fate and that she had not underestimated the dark side of the Force.

==Continuity==
In the ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' episode "[[Twilight of the Apprentice]]" [[Ahsoka Tano]] reads a phrase "taka zeech ma toka duuwaj" in the [[Ur-Kittât]] from an obelisk.<ref name="Pablo Tweet">{{Twitter|pabl0hidalgo|status/1399396084078682113|Pablo Hidalgo|quote=This may be the deepest cut in recent memory.|archivedate=20210531174752}}</ref>
